theoremTarget.name: Rescue Lemma
theoremTarget.statement: There exist constants K > 0 and C0 such that every C >= C0 has some integer g with 0 <= g <= K log^2(C) and both C-g and C+g prime.
theoremTarget.goldbachImplication: If the Rescue Lemma is proven analytically and the finite base below C0 is verified, then Goldbach follows.

lemmaStatus.Lemma 5 finite base: FINITE EVIDENCE, local run reported passed

finiteBaseStatus.scope: intended exhaustive check for every integer center 2 <= C < 100000
finiteBaseStatus.safeInterpretation: finite base check passed in the reported local run

mirrorDesertBreak.p95K: stayed low in most tested bands
mirrorDesertBreak.maxK: K=12 had zero misses in the completed K-window packets so far
mirrorDesertBreak.desertPattern: deserts grew with scale but still broke
mirrorDesertBreak.halfHitPattern: half-hit sparks appeared before most rescues
mirrorDesertBreak.lanePattern: first-rescue residue lanes became more diverse in higher bands

sparkAndDensity.sparkRate: decayed with scale in finite tests

adversarialEvidenceSummary.smallPrimeWheels: tested wheels through levels such as 510510
adversarialEvidenceSummary.coverage: over 95% of gaps blocked in hardest tested cases

openAnalyticGap.neededBound: Prove S(C,W) = sum_{0 <= g <= W} 1_prime(C-g) * 1_prime(C+g) > 0 for every sufficiently large C, with W = K log^2(C).
openAnalyticGap.reviewerFacingWall: Prove that the survivor gaps left after local congruence obstructions contain at least one true BOTH-prime hit inside a K log^2(C) mirror window.
openAnalyticGap.importantCorrection: Brun-Titchmarsh is an upper bound, not a lower bound; it cannot prove the Rescue Lemma by itself.

unsafeClaimsToAvoid: Claiming finite validation proves the theorem.
Claiming the universal proof is complete.
safeClaimsToUse: Finite tests strongly support the Rescue Lemma target.
The Rescue Lemma is computationally supported and analytically open.
The current framework is a proof-direction framework.
